content.full_text:
841102

February 2, 1978

Sig Sanchez
Supervisor, 1st District
Board of Supervisors
Room 524 County Administration Building
70 West Hedding
San Jose, California 95110

Dear Mr. Sanchez:

This letter is in response to your letter to the Commission dated
January 19, 1978 regarding the disposition of surplus funds by a
committee.

You have inquired as to whether there are any restrictions on how
surplus funds of the committee may be disposed of. The Political
Reform Act does not place any restriction on the disposition of
surplus funds; however, a committee that receives contributions of
$500 or more in a calendar year is required to file a Statement of
Organization pursuant to Government Code Section 84102, and include
a description of how surplus funds will be disposed of in the event of
dissolution. Whenever there is a change in any information contained in the Statement of Organization, including the description
of how surplus funds will be disposed of, an amendment is required
to be filed within 10 days to reflect the change. If you cannot
recall what you stated on the Statement of Organization at the time
you filed with the Secretary of State's Office, you may either call
or write the Secretary of State, P.O. Box 1467, Sacramento 95807 or
916/323-4880 and they will be able to give you that information from
the original document. If you dispose of your surplus funds and do
not withdraw any additional activity concern this fund you
should send a termination statement to the Secretary of State's Office.

Enclosed for your use and information is a termination form and
fact sheet which outline the conditions under which a committee may
terminate. I trust this information will be of assistance to you.
Please let me know if you require additional information.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchina
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

Enclosure

BOARD OF SUPERVISORS
COUNTY OF SANTA CLARA
ROOM 524 COUNTY ADMINISTRATION BUILDING
70 WEST HEDDING ST. SAN JOSE, CALIFORNIA 95110 298-6323

SIG SANCHEZ
SUPERVISOR FIRST DISTRICT

January 19, 1978

Fair Political Practices Division
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlepersons:

There is some doubt at the present time as to whether I am
going to be a candidate for re-election for the office of
Supervisor for the First Supervisorial District in the County
of Santa Clara.

I have some $800.00 plus left over in my campaign fund.
What alternatives do I have for disposition of these funds?
May I have some guidelines or examples thereof as to what
can be done with these funds.

Any information on the above would be greatly appreciated.

Sincerely,

SIG SANCHEZ
Supervisor First District

SS/dgh
